qa_pairs.question: What is the primary focus of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page explains issues related to transferring your data between our facilities and our regional partners.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have any doubts about the advice provided on data transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are in any doubt about the advice provided, contact our technical support for help.

qa_pairs.question: What should I clarify before starting a data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Before migrating, you should clarify whether you are responsible for your own data migration or if our staff will be migrating your data.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I haven't used Globus before a data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: If you haven't used Globus before, you should read about it now and verify that it works on the system you are migrating from.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended practice for other data migration tools like tar, gzip, or zip?
qa_pairs.answer: You should test any other tools you plan to use, such as tar, gzip, or zip, on test data to ensure you know how they work before using them on important information.

qa_pairs.question: When is the best time to start a data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: You should not wait until the last minute to start your data migration.

qa_pairs.question: How long should I expect large data transfers to take?
qa_pairs.answer: You should expect hundreds of gigabytes to take hours and terabytes to take days, and it's wise to give yourself days in case problems arise.

qa_pairs.question: What factors can influence the duration of a data transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: The duration of a data transfer can depend on the amount of data, and the load on the machines and network.

qa_pairs.question: Why is cleaning up files important before a data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: A major data migration is a good reminder to clean up your files and directories because moving less data takes less time, and storage space on new systems is in great demand.

qa_pairs.question: What types of files can often be deleted during a cleanup before migration?
qa_pairs.answer: You can delete intermediate files if you compile programs (using commands like `make clean`, `make realclean`, or `rm *.o`) and large core dump files (like `core.12345`) if you don't know what they are.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it more efficient to archive many small files into one before transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: Most file transfer programs move one file of a reasonable size more efficiently than thousands of small files of equal total size.

qa_pairs.question: Which tool is recommended for archiving directories with many small files?
qa_pairs.answer: `tar` is recommended to combine (archive) directories or directory trees containing many small files.

qa_pairs.question: When can large files benefit from compression during transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: Large files, especially text files, can benefit from compression in some cases when transferring them.

qa_pairs.question: Does compressing a file for transfer always save time?
qa_pairs.answer: No, compressing a file solely for the purpose of transferring it and then decompressing it at the end will not necessarily save time.

qa_pairs.question: What factors determine if compressing a file for transfer is worthwhile?
qa_pairs.answer: Whether compressing a file saves time depends on how much the file can be compressed, how long it takes to compress it, and the transfer bandwidth.

qa_pairs.question: What tools can be used for compression if it is deemed worthwhile for a transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: If compression is deemed worthwhile, you can use `tar` or `gzip` for the task.

qa_pairs.question: What should I avoid regarding data duplication when migrating from multiple source systems to one new system?
qa_pairs.answer: You should avoid moving the same data twice. If data is duplicated across multiple source systems, choose one system and only move the duplicate data from that single source.

qa_pairs.question: What precaution should I take regarding files with duplicate names during migration?
qa_pairs.answer: You should be aware of files with duplicate names that do not contain duplicate information and ensure that you will not accidentally overwrite one file with another of the same name.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended tool for file transfer during migration if supported at the source site?
qa_pairs.answer: If supported at your source site, `Globus` is the recommended tool to set up your file transfer during migration.

qa_pairs.question: Why is Globus considered efficient and user-friendly for file transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus is considered the most user-friendly and efficient tool for file transfers because it is designed to recover from network interruptions automatically.

qa_pairs.question: What specific setting is recommended to enable when using Globus for transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to enable the setting to 'preserve source file modification times' in the 'Transfer & Timer Options' when using Globus.

qa_pairs.question: What becomes more important if Globus is not supported at the source site?
qa_pairs.answer: If Globus is not supported at your source site, then compressing data and avoiding duplication become even more important.

qa_pairs.question: What strategy should be used when migrating data with tools like scp, sftp, or rsync?
qa_pairs.answer: If using scp, sftp, or rsync, you should create a schedule to migrate your data in blocks of a few hundreds of gigabytes at a time.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it beneficial to migrate data in blocks when using scp, sftp, or rsync?
qa_pairs.answer: Migrating data in blocks allows you to restart from an incomplete file if the transfer stops, without having to re-transfer files that are already complete.
